# Sub 16: Extract SwarmHeartbeatCoordinatorService — getDueTask only, initAsync stays in service

## Parent Epic

#11831 — Sub 16 of Epic. Second of the 3 coordinator extractions in Discussion #11857 Cycle-3.5 B-prime convergence.

## Premise

Per `v13-path.md:117` D3.1 boundary, per-task coordinators decide due. `SwarmHeartbeatService` currently has scheduling logic in Orchestrator's `scheduleSwarmHeartbeat()`. Extract to coordinator.

**Specific care (per GPT Cycle-1.1):** `SwarmHeartbeatService.initAsync()` is boot/lifecycle setup (one-time init for the pulse loop). It is NOT a scheduler concern and MUST stay in `SwarmHeartbeatService`. Coordinator owns ONLY due-trigger selection.

## Prescription

Create `ai/daemons/orchestrator/services/SwarmHeartbeatCoordinatorService.mjs`:

```js
getDueTask({state, now, swarmHeartbeatIntervalMs, ...}) → trigger | null
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. `ai/daemons/orchestrator/services/SwarmHeartbeatCoordinatorService.mjs` ships with `getDueTask({...})` only
2. Pure-function unit tests for `getDueTask({...})` with synthetic state
3. `SwarmHeartbeatService` retains `initAsync()`, `pulse()`, and all lifecycle/execution methods unchanged
4. Orchestrator's `scheduleSwarmHeartbeat()` body is NOT modified yet (Sub 18 wires)
5. No new env vars / config keys / behavior changes

## Avoided Traps

- `initAsync()` MUST stay in `SwarmHeartbeatService` — coordinator absorbing it is the anti-pattern
- No reactive-config-aliased wrapper
- No premature Orchestrator integration

## Depends on

Epic #11831.

## Unblocks

Sub 18.

## Authority

Discussion #11857 Cycle-3.5 graduation.

**Authored by:** [Claude Opus 4.7] (Claude Code)

* feat(agentos): extract DreamCoordinatorService — getDueTask only (#11858)

Authored by Claude Opus 4.7 (Claude Code). Session 5572d9a5-558d-4bea-b416-e31496c289c4.

FAIR-band: in-band [verify @ merge-gate]

Resolves #11858

Sub 15 of Epic #11831 — first of three coordinator extractions in Discussion
#11857 Cycle-3.5 B-prime convergence (graduated 2026-05-23T19:23Z, GPT
[GRADUATION_APPROVED] DC_kwDODSospM4BA-...). Matches D3.1 boundary per
v13-path.md:117: per-task coordinators decide what work is due; orchestrator
wires; ProcessSupervisor executes.

Creates `ai/daemons/orchestrator/services/DreamCoordinatorService.mjs`:
- Pure-function `buildDreamTrigger({now, lastRunAt, intervalMs})` returns
  `{taskName, source, reason}` or null
- `DreamCoordinatorService.getDueTask({state, now, dreamIntervalMs})` wraps
  the pure function with state-shape mapping
- Mirrors `SummarizationCoordinatorService.getDueTask({...})` precedent shape

Strict boundary preserved:
- DreamService retains `processUndigestedSessions()` and all execution-side
  methods unchanged — coordinator owns due-trigger selection ONLY
- No state writes / no markStarted/markCompleted/recordTaskOutcome calls
- No reactive-config-aliased wrapper around DreamService (Sub-1 anti-pattern
  at new layer is explicitly avoided per Discussion #11857 Avoided Traps)
- Orchestrator's scheduleDreamCycle() body unchanged in this Sub; Sub 18
  #11862 wires the new coordinator into the registry

Pure-function unit tests: 8/8 pass covering interval-elapsed boundaries,
disabled (`intervalMs <= 0`), exactly-equal-to-interval edge, reason-carries-
interval-verbatim for telemetry, getDueTask state mapping (including
graceful missing-state default to lastRunAt=0).

* fix(agentos): switch DreamCoordinatorService to gatekeep pattern (#11858)

Same anti-pattern operator caught earlier today on Env (Sub 1 review):
class extending Base + singleton: true + Neo.setupClass for a service that
uses ZERO Neo features — no reactive configs, no lifecycle hooks, no
construct/apply/createId, no className/singleton semantics actually used.
Just a namespace for one pure-wrapper method around buildDreamTrigger.

Switched to gatekeep pattern per src/core/IdGenerator.mjs + src/util/Env.mjs
precedent: plain object literal + Neo.gatekeep(obj, 'Neo.x.y.Z'). Drops
Base inheritance overhead + setupClass processing + the empty static config.

Consumer surface unchanged (DreamCoordinatorService.getDueTask({...}) works
identically). 8/8 unit tests pass against the new shape.

Precedent debt acknowledged: SummarizationCoordinatorService /
BackupCoordinatorService / PrimaryRepoSyncService all have the same
anti-pattern (Base + singleton: true for stateless utilities). Worth a
follow-up sweep — flagged via spawn_task in this turn for separate cleanup.

* refactor(agentos): collapse DreamCoordinator's pure-fn/wrapper split — one getDueTask (#11858)

Operator caught the split as ceremony-without-payoff. Honest answer: I
mimicked SummarizationCoordinatorService's pattern (separate buildSummaryTrigger
pure function + getDueTask wrapper) without challenging whether the split
was earned. There it IS earned — SummarizationCoordinator.getDueTask calls
getUnreadSunsetHandoversFn(db) for additional I/O input + attaches an
onSuccess hook that marks handovers as read. The pure function takes 4
inputs across 2 branches; the wrapper does real extra work.

DreamCoordinator's getDueTask just maps state.lastRunAt ?? 0 into the
pure function's arg. No I/O, no side-effect hooks, no extra branches.
The split adds ceremony, not value.

Collapsed to single getDueTask({state, now, dreamIntervalMs}). Spec
collapses 8 → 5 tests (the redundant pure-vs-wrapper duplicates
disappear). Default export still uses Neo.gatekeep so the registry
descriptor shape (coordinator field with .getDueTask method) works
identically.

* refactor(agentos): DreamCoordinator pure module — drop gatekeep + default export (#11858)

Two issues operator caught on re-read:

1. Stale namespace path. Both the @namespace JSDoc tag and the Neo.gatekeep
   argument referenced `Neo.ai.daemons.services.DreamCoordinatorService`
   (the pre-Sub-8 path). Actual filesystem location is now
   `ai/daemons/orchestrator/services/` → namespace would be
   `Neo.ai.daemons.orchestrator.services.DreamCoordinatorService`. Copied
   stale JSDoc from SummarizationCoord precedent without updating.

2. Double export of the same function. `getDueTask` was exported both as
   a named export AND wrapped in the gatekeep default-export object. One
   function exposed via two import paths = same ceremony-without-payoff
   anti-pattern from the prior commit, just at a different layer.

Deeper question: why register via Neo.gatekeep at all? Env / IdGenerator
use it because Neo.util.Env / Neo.core.IdGenerator are broadly-used
substrate where tooling/debug access via Neo.ns() has real value.
DreamCoordinator is orchestrator-internal — consumers import the function
directly; namespace registration buys nothing.

Final shape: pure module with one named export. No gatekeep. No default.
No @namespace JSDoc. Spec drops the Neo + core/_export bootstrap imports
too (not needed without module-load gatekeep call).

4/4 spec tests pass. Net diff vs prior commit: -36 LOC.

Sub 18's registry will consume via `import {getDueTask as dreamGetDueTask}
from '.../DreamCoordinatorService.mjs'` and register the function on a
descriptor — coordinator-as-object wrapper not needed.

suffixes imply state + methods + lifecycle; this file holds one pure
trigger projection. Files describe what they are.

  -> test/.../scheduling/dream.spec.mjs

New folder scheduling/ (sibling to services/) holds per-task due-trigger
projections. services/ keeps things that are genuine services. Example:
SummarizationCoordinatorService calls getUnreadSunsetHandoversFn(db) and
attaches onSuccess hooks; that one EARNED the Service suffix. DreamService
/ SwarmHeartbeatService / GoldenPathSynthesizer stay in services/ as
execution-side responsibilities.

JSDoc + describe title updated. No more Coordinator/Service in this file
or its spec. 4/4 spec tests pass at new location.

Sub 18's registry will consume via:
  import {getDueTask as dreamGetDueTask} from './scheduling/dream.mjs'

Downstream churn flagged: Discussion #11857 body + tickets #11859/11860
still use ...CoordinatorService naming. Will update when filing Sub 16 /
Sub 17 work (same shape: scheduling/<task>.mjs). Sub 19 #11861 stays
Service-named since MaintenanceBackpressureService IS a genuine service
(state + multiple visibly-separate surfaces + lease delegation).

* docs(agentos): drop decay-prone JSDoc references in scheduling/dream.mjs (#11858)

Operator: "we e.g. change the referenced file, and then what? history versus
intents."

Removed brittle cross-references that lie when targets shift:
- v13-path.md:117 (line number — lies on any edit above line 117)
- D3.1 boundary (section label — lies on doc restructure)
- DreamService.processUndigestedSessions() (specific method name — lies on rename)
- @see ai/daemons/orchestrator/services/DreamService.mjs (scaffolding the
  reader can infer from file location + function name)

Same anti-pattern as Sub 1's historical-context comments: describes a
moment-in-time architecture state, not the file's own intent. JSDoc
should describe what THIS file IS so it stays true regardless of
neighboring substrate evolution.

Result: JSDoc now describes the function's contract (returns trigger
when interval elapsed since lastRunAt; pure). No external coordinates.
4/4 spec tests still pass.

Lesson for Sub 16/17/18/19: no baked line numbers, no section labels,
no neighboring-file method names in JSDoc prose.

* docs(agentos): collapse duplicate JSDoc blocks — one function, one comment (#11858)

Operator: file has ONE function, two comment blocks said the same thing
twice. File-level @summary made sense when this was a class with methods
(class JSDoc + method JSDoc). Now that it's a single pure function, the
file IS the function — one JSDoc above getDueTask covers both.

4/4 spec tests still pass.

- 2026-05-23T20:42:53Z @neo-opus-4-7 cross-referenced by PR #11865
- 2026-05-23T20:50:21Z @tobiu referenced in commit `ac2fecf` - "feat(agentos): extract swarm-heartbeat due-trigger projection (#11859) (#11865)

Sub 16 of Epic #11831. Pure function in scheduling/<task>.mjs, matching the
shape Sub 15 (PR #11863) established after 6 iterations of operator-led
KISS pressure. Applies all Sub-15 lessons from the start:

- File at scheduling/swarmHeartbeat.mjs (no Service/Coordinator suffix)
- One JSDoc block above the one function (no file-level + function-level split)
- No class extends Base, no singleton config, no Neo.gatekeep
- No decay-prone JSDoc references (no v13-path.md:N, no D3.1, no method names)
- Spec doesn't import Neo (no module-load gatekeep call requiring globalThis.Neo)
- Function signature parallel to scheduling/dream.mjs

4/4 spec tests pass.

Orchestrator's inline schedule logic (lines 906-934) is NOT modified yet —
Sub 18 #11862 wires the new projection into the registry. SwarmHeartbeatService
retains initAsync() + pulse() + all lifecycle/execution methods. The
enabled-gate (this.swarmHeartbeatEnabled) stays in Orchestrator's call-site
until Sub 18 moves profile-aware filtering into pickNextCandidate.
